inline_review:
commit a95fa11e83e2a11d1c547b2f2da9f6363ccc3258
Author: Li Wang <liwang@redhat.com>

selftests/mm: skip hugetlb_dio tests when DIO alignment is incompatible

This commit adds DIO alignment checks to hugetlb_dio tests using statx
and STATX_DIOALIGN. This prevents test failures on filesystems that
require specific alignments, and reuses a single file descriptor across
all test cases.

> diff --git a/tools/testing/selftests/mm/hugetlb_dio.c b/tools/testing/selftests/mm/hugetlb_dio.c
> index 9ac62eb4c97d..1c673c6c685e 100644
> --- a/tools/testing/selftests/mm/hugetlb_dio.c
> +++ b/tools/testing/selftests/mm/hugetlb_dio.c

[ ... ]

> +static int get_dio_alignment(int fd)
> +{
> +	struct statx stx;
> +	int ret;
> +
> +	ret = syscall(__NR_statx, fd, "", AT_EMPTY_PATH, STATX_DIOALIGN, &stx);
> +	if (ret < 0)
> +		return -1;
> +
> +	/*
> +	 * If STATX_DIOALIGN is unsupported, assume no alignment
> +	 * constraint and let the test proceed.
> +	 */
> +	if (!(stx.stx_mask & STATX_DIOALIGN) || !stx.stx_dio_offset_align)
> +		return 1;
> +
> +	return stx.stx_dio_offset_align;
> +}

The test includes <sys/stat.h>, which fetches the struct statx definition
from the system's C library. On systems with older glibc (like < 2.37,
e.g., Ubuntu 22.04 LTS), will the compiler fail here because struct statx
doesn't have the stx_dio_offset_align member?

Even though STATX_DIOALIGN is defined as a fallback above, is there a way
to handle the missing struct member so this doesn't break the build on
older environments?

> +static bool check_dio_alignment(unsigned int start_off,
> +			        unsigned int end_off, unsigned int align)
> +{
> +	unsigned int writesize = end_off - start_off;
> +
> +	/*
> +	 * The kernel's DIO path checks that file offset, length, and
> +	 * buffer address are all multiples of dio_offset_align.  When
> +	 * this test case's parameters don't satisfy that, the write
> +	 * would fail with -EINVAL before exercising the hugetlb unpin
> +	 * path, so skip.
> +	 */
> +	if (start_off % align != 0 || writesize % align != 0) {
> +		ksft_test_result_skip("DIO align=%u incompatible with offset %u writesize %u\n",
> +				align, start_off, writesize);
> +		return false;
> +	}

Does this skip valid test cases by checking the memory buffer alignment
(start_off) against the file offset alignment (stx_dio_offset_align)?

The statx system call provides two distinct fields for direct I/O alignment:
stx_dio_mem_align (for user-space memory buffer addresses) and
stx_dio_offset_align (for file offsets and lengths).

If a filesystem requires a 4096-byte alignment for lengths but allows a
512-byte alignment for memory buffers, a test case with a memory buffer
offset of 2048 could be valid but skipped here because 2048 % 4096 != 0.

Should start_off be checked against stx_dio_mem_align instead?
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"Compilation fails on systems with older glibc (e.g., < 2.37) because `struct statx` in `<sys/stat.h>` lacks the `stx_dio_offset_align` field.","severity":"Low","severity_explanation":"The test includes `<sys/stat.h>`, which fetches the `struct statx` definition from the system's C library. The `stx_dio_offset_align` field was added to the kernel in 6.1 and to glibc in 2.37. On systems with older glibc (like Ubuntu 22.04 LTS), the compiler will fail with `error: 'struct statx' has no member named 'stx_dio_offset_align'`. While the patch attempts to polyfill the macro `STATX_DIOALIGN`, it fails to provide a fallback struct definition for the missing field. Build issues are classified as Low severity."},{"problem":"The test uses the file offset alignment (`stx_dio_offset_align`) to validate the memory buffer alignment (`start_off`), which is imprecise and can cause valid tests to be skipped.","severity":"Medium","severity_explanation":"The `statx` system call provides two distinct fields for direct I/O alignment: `stx_dio_mem_align` (for user-space memory buffer addresses) and `stx_dio_offset_align` (for file offsets and lengths). The patch incorrectly checks both `start_off % align` and `writesize % align` against `stx_dio_offset_align`. If a filesystem requires a 4096-byte alignment for lengths but allows a 512-byte alignment for memory buffers, a valid test case with `start_off` = 2048 and `writesize` = 12288 will be incorrectly skipped because `2048 % 4096 != 0`. The memory buffer alignment (`start_off`) should be checked against `stx_dio_mem_align`. Issues in kselftests logic are Medium severity."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit a95fa11e83e2a11d1c547b2f2da9f6363ccc3258
